generated_by: pna-build-eval-contrib skill (illustrative sample — fictional candidate, not a real app)

summary.headline: Private data leaves the device: notes are POSTed to a vendor analytics endpoint (AC-1), and an LLM 'smart compose' calls a cloud model over private fields with no consent gate and no declared exception (AC-PRM-A). Not safe to trust with relationship data.

findings.requirement: Shared data MUST be read-only and externally managed; private data MUST be read-write and locally owned. The two stores MUST occupy separate namespaces and private data MUST NOT leave the device on its own.

citations.note: POSTs note bodies to https://telemetry.vendor.example/v1/events on every edit

evidence.detail: Off-device egress to telemetry.vendor.example is not on any allow-list (AC-1 violation).
The payload includes record_notes rows — private data by definition — so this is a Goal-1 sovereignty violation, not anonymous-counter telemetry.

findings.requirement: Any LLM invocation over Private or Shared data MUST be treated as a transport: a local model is the default; a cloud model MUST be opt-in per call; the user MUST see the prompt and any merged data before send.

citations.note: sends private fields to a cloud completion API on keystroke — no consent, no preview

findings.rationale: Undeclared deviation: the app departs from the local-only guarantee without raising EX-CLOUD-LLM (no consent gate, no persistent non-PNA-mode signal). It should have declared and handled that exception; absent that, this is a silent (uncaught) conformance failure — the backstop in SKILL.md § Evaluate flow.

evidence.detail: No EX-* attestation in the source and no runtime consent surface; names the EX-CLOUD-LLM exception it should have raised.

citations.note: an IndexedDB snapshot ring exists (durability is bounded by the evictable substrate, but the backup path is present)
